1	/*!
2	 *  Copyright (c) 2023-2025 by Contributors
3	 * \file serve/engine.h
4	 * \brief The header of serving engine in MLC LLM.
5	 */
6	#ifndef MLC_LLM_SERVE_ENGINE_H_
7	#define MLC_LLM_SERVE_ENGINE_H_
8	
9	#include "data.h"
10	#include "engine_state.h"
11	#include "event_trace_recorder.h"
12	#include "request.h"
13	#include "request_state.h"
14	
15	namespace mlc {
16	namespace llm {
17	namespace serve {
18	
19	using namespace tvm::runtime;
20	
21	class Engine;
22	
23	/*!
24	 * \brief The output of engine creation, including the created engine and
25	 * the default generation config for requests.
26	 */
27	struct EngineCreationOutput {
28	  std::unique_ptr<Engine> reloaded_engine;
29	  EngineConfig completed_engine_config;
30	  GenerationConfig default_generation_cfg;
31	};
32	
33	/*!
34	 * \brief The engine interface for request serving in MLC LLM.
35	 * The engine can run one or multiple LLM models internally for
36	 * text generation. Usually, when there are multiple models,
37	 * speculative inference will be activated, where the first model
38	 * (index 0) is the main "large model" that has better generation
39	 * quality, and all other models are "small" models that used for
40	 * speculation.
41	 * The engine receives requests from the "AddRequest" method. For
42	 * an given request, the engine will keep generating new tokens for
43	 * the request until finish (under certain criterion). After finish,
44	 * the engine will return the generation result through the callback
45	 * function provided by the request.
46	 * \note For now only one model run in the engine is supported.
47	 * Multiple model support such as speculative inference will
48	 * be followed soon in the future.
49	 *
50	 * The public interface of Engine has the following three categories:
51	 * - engine management,
52	 * - high-level request management,
53	 * - engine "step" action.
54	 */
55	class Engine {
56	 public:
57	  /********************** Engine Management **********************/
58	  virtual ~Engine() = default;
59	
60	  /*!
61	   * \brief Create an engine in unique pointer.
62	   * \param engine_config_json_str The serialized JSON string of the engine config.
63	   * \param device The device where the run models.
64	   * \param request_stream_callback The request stream callback function to.
65	   * \param trace_recorder Event trace recorder for requests.
66	   * \return The created Engine in pointer, and the default generation config.
67	   */
68	  static Result<EngineCreationOutput> Create(const std::string& engine_config_json_str,
69	                                             Device device,
70	                                             FRequestStreamCallback request_stream_callback,
71	                                             Optional<EventTraceRecorder> trace_recorder);
72	
73	  /*! \brief Reset the engine, clean up all running data and metrics. */
74	  virtual void Reset() = 0;
75	
76	  /*! \brief Check if the engine has no request to process. */
77	  virtual bool Empty() = 0;
78	
79	  /*! \brief Get the request stream callback function of the engine. */
80	  virtual FRequestStreamCallback GetRequestStreamCallback() = 0;
81	
82	  /*! \brief Set the request stream callback function of the engine. */
83	  virtual void SetRequestStreamCallback(FRequestStreamCallback request_stream_callback) = 0;
84	
85	  /***************** High-level Request Management *****************/
86	
87	  /*! \brief Add a new request to the engine. */
88	  virtual void AddRequest(Request request) = 0;
89	
90	  /*! \brief Abort the input request (specified by id string) from engine. */
91	  virtual void AbortRequest(const String& request_id) = 0;
92	
93	  /*! \brief Abort all requests from the engine. */
94	  virtual void AbortAllRequests() = 0;
95	
96	  /*********************** Engine Action ***********************/
97	
98	  /*!
99	   * \brief The main function that the engine takes a step of action.
100	   * At each step, the engine may decide to
101	   * - run prefill for one (or more) requests,
102	   * - run one-step decode for the all existing requests
103	   * ...
104	   * In the end of certain actions (e.g., decode), the engine will
105	   * check if any request has finished, and will return the
106	   * generation results for those finished requests.
107	   */
108	  virtual void Step() = 0;
109	
110	  /************** Debug/Profile **************/
111	
112	  /*! \brief Internal engine metrics. */
113	  virtual String JSONMetrics() = 0;
114	
115	  /*! \brief Call the given global function on all workers. Only for debug purpose. */
116	  virtual void DebugCallFuncOnAllAllWorker(const String& func_name, Optional<String> func_args) = 0;
117	};
118	
119	void AbortRequestImpl(EngineState estate, const Array<Model>& models, const String& request_id,
120	                      String finish_reason = "abort");
121	
122	}  // namespace serve
123	}  // namespace llm
124	}  // namespace mlc
125	
126	#endif  // MLC_LLM_SERVE_ENGINE_H_
127

1	/*!
2	 *  Copyright (c) 2023-2025 by Contributors
3	 * \file serve/threaded_engine.h
4	 * \brief The header of threaded serving engine in MLC LLM.
5	 */
6	#ifndef MLC_LLM_SERVE_THREADED_ENGINE_H_
7	#define MLC_LLM_SERVE_THREADED_ENGINE_H_
8	
9	#include "data.h"
10	#include "engine.h"
11	#include "request.h"
12	
13	namespace mlc {
14	namespace llm {
15	namespace serve {
16	
17	using namespace tvm::runtime;
18	
19	/*!
20	 * \brief The interface threaded engine in MLC LLM.
21	 * The threaded engine keeps running a background request processing
22	 * loop on a standalone thread. Ensuring thread safety, it exposes
23	 * `AddRequest` and `AbortRequest` to receive new requests or
24	 * abortions from other threads, and the internal request processing
25	 * is backed by a normal engine wrapped inside.
26	 */
27	class ThreadedEngine {
28	 public:
29	  /*! \brief Create a ThreadedEngine. */
30	  static std::unique_ptr<ThreadedEngine> Create();
31	
32	  virtual ~ThreadedEngine() = default;
33	
34	  /*!
35	   * \brief Initialize the threaded engine from packed arguments in PackedArgs.
36	   * \param device The device where to run models.
37	   * \param request_stream_callback The request stream callback function to.
38	   * \param trace_recorder Event trace recorder for requests.
39	   */
40	  virtual void InitThreadedEngine(Device device, Optional<Function> request_stream_callback,
41	                                  Optional<EventTraceRecorder> trace_recorder) = 0;
42	
43	  /*!
44	   * \brief Reload the engine with the new engine config.
45	   * \param engine_config_json_str The engine config JSON string.
46	   */
47	  virtual void Reload(String engine_config_json_str) = 0;
48	
49	  /*! \brief Unload the background engine. */
50	  virtual void Unload() = 0;
51	
52	  /*! \brief Reset the engine to the initial state. */
53	  virtual void Reset() = 0;
54	
55	  /*! \brief Starts the background request processing loop. */
56	  virtual void RunBackgroundLoop() = 0;
57	
58	  /*! \brief Starts the request stream callback loop. */
59	  virtual void RunBackgroundStreamBackLoop() = 0;
60	
61	  /*!
62	   * \brief Notify the ThreadedEngine to exit the background
63	   * request processing loop. This method is invoked by threads
64	   * other than the engine-driving thread.
65	   */
66	  virtual void ExitBackgroundLoop() = 0;
67	
68	  /*! \brief Add a new request to the engine. */
69	  virtual void AddRequest(Request request) = 0;
70	
71	  /*! \brief Abort the input request (specified by id string) from engine. */
72	  virtual void AbortRequest(const String& request_id) = 0;
73	
74	  /************** Query/Profile/Debug **************/
75	
76	  /*! \brief Return the default generation config. */
77	  virtual GenerationConfig GetDefaultGenerationConfig() const = 0;
78	
79	  /*! \brief Return the complete engine config. */
80	  virtual EngineConfig GetCompleteEngineConfig() const = 0;
81	
82	  /*! \brief Call the given global function on all workers. Only for debug purpose. */
83	  virtual void DebugCallFuncOnAllAllWorker(const String& func_name, Optional<String> func_args) = 0;
84	};
85	
86	}  // namespace serve
87	}  // namespace llm
88	}  // namespace mlc
89	
90	#endif  // MLC_LLM_SERVE_THREADED_ENGINE_H_
91

1	/*!
2	 *  Copyright (c) 2023-2025 by Contributors
3	 * \file serve/engine_actions/action.h
4	 * \brief The abstraction of actions (e.g., prefill/decode) that an
5	 * Engine can take at each time step.
6	 */
7	#ifndef MLC_LLM_SERVE_ENGINE_ACTIONS_ACTION_H_
8	#define MLC_LLM_SERVE_ENGINE_ACTIONS_ACTION_H_
9	
10	#include "../config.h"
11	#include "../draft_token_workspace_manager.h"
12	#include "../engine.h"
13	#include "../engine_state.h"
14	#include "../event_trace_recorder.h"
15	#include "../model.h"
16	#include "../sampler/sampler.h"
17	
18	namespace mlc {
19	namespace llm {
20	namespace serve {
21	
22	using namespace tvm::runtime;
23	using tvm::ffi::Object;
24	using tvm::ffi::ObjectRef;
25	
26	/*!
27	 * \brief The abstraction of actions that an Engine can take at each time step.
28	 * The only core interface of an action is the `Step` function.
29	 * At high level, the Step function takes the current engine state
30	 * as input, invokes model functions (such as batched-prefill or
31	 * batched-decode), run sampler to sample new tokens, and update
32	 * the engine state.
33	 */
34	class EngineActionObj : public Object {
35	 public:
36	  /*!
37	   * \brief The behavior of the engine action in a single step.
38	   * \param estate The engine state to be analyzed and updated.
39	   * \return The processed requests in this step.
40	   */
41	  virtual Array<Request> Step(EngineState estate) = 0;
42	
43	  static void RegisterReflection() {
44	    namespace refl = tvm::ffi::reflection;
45	    refl::ObjectDef<EngineActionObj>();
46	  }
47	
48	  static constexpr const bool _type_has_method_sequal_reduce = false;
49	  static constexpr const bool _type_has_method_shash_reduce = false;
50	  static constexpr const bool _type_mutable = true;
51	  TVM_FFI_DECLARE_OBJECT_INFO("mlc.serve.EngineAction", EngineActionObj, Object);
52	};
53	
54	/*!
55	 * \brief Managed reference of EngineActionObj.
56	 * It declares the full list of supported actions.
57	 * \sa EngineActionObj
58	 */
59	class EngineAction : public ObjectRef {
60	 public:
61	  /*!
62	   * \brief Create the action that prefills requests in the `waiting_queue`
63	   * of the engine state.
64	   * \param models The models to run prefill in.
65	   * \param logit_processor The logit processor.
66	   * \param sampler The sampler to sample new tokens.
67	   * \param model_workspaces The workspace of each model.
68	   * \param engine_config The engine config.
69	   * \param model_configs The config of each model.
70	   * \param trace_recorder The event trace recorder for requests.
71	   * \return The created action object.
72	   */
73	  static EngineAction NewRequestPrefill(Array<Model> models, LogitProcessor logit_processor,
74	                                        Sampler sampler,
75	                                        std::vector<ModelWorkspace> model_workspaces,
76	                                        EngineConfig engine_config,
77	                                        std::vector<tvm::ffi::json::Object> model_configs,
78	                                        Optional<EventTraceRecorder> trace_recorder);
79	  /*!
80	   * \brief Create the action that prefills requests in the `waiting_queue`
81	   * of the engine state.
82	   * \param models The models to run prefill in.
83	   * \param logit_processor The logit processor.
84	   * \param sampler The sampler to sample new tokens.
85	   * \param model_workspaces The workspace of each model.
86	   * \param draft_token_workspace_manager The draft token workspace manager.
87	   * \param engine_config The engine config.
88	   * \param model_configs The config of each model.
89	   * \param trace_recorder The event trace recorder for requests.
90	   * \return The created action object.
91	   */
92	  static EngineAction EagleNewRequestPrefill(
93	      Array<Model> models, LogitProcessor logit_processor, Sampler sampler,
94	      std::vector<ModelWorkspace> model_workspaces,
95	      DraftTokenWorkspaceManager draft_token_workspace_manager, EngineConfig engine_config,
96	      std::vector<tvm::ffi::json::Object> model_configs,
97	      Optional<EventTraceRecorder> trace_recorder);
98	  /*!
99	   * \brief Create the action that runs one-step decode for requests in the
100	   * `running_queue` of engine state. Preempt low-priority requests
101	   * accordingly when it is impossible to decode all the running requests.
102	   * \note The BatchDecode action **does not** take effect for speculative
103	   * decoding scenarios where there are multiple models. For speculative
104	   * decoding in the future, we will use other specific actions.
105	   * \param models The model to run decode in. When there are multiple
106	   * models, the `Step` function of the created action will not take effect.
107	   * \param tokenizer The tokenizer of the engine.
108	   * \param sampler The sampler to sample new tokens.
109	   * \param engine_config The engine config.
110	   * \param trace_recorder The event trace recorder for requests.
111	   * \return The created action object.
112	   */
113	  static EngineAction BatchDecode(Array<Model> models, Tokenizer tokenizer,
114	                                  LogitProcessor logit_processor, Sampler sampler,
115	                                  EngineConfig engine_config,
116	                                  Optional<EventTraceRecorder> trace_recorder);
117	
118	  /*!
119	   * \brief Create the action that runs one-step speculative draft proposal for
120	   * requests in the `running_queue` of engine state. Preempt low-priority requests
121	   * accordingly when it is impossible to decode all the running requests.
122	   * \param models The model to run decode in. When there are multiple
123	   * models, the `Step` function of the created action will not take effect.
124	   * \param sampler The sampler to sample new tokens.
125	   * \param model_workspaces The workspace of each model.
126	   * \param draft_token_workspace_manager The draft token workspace manager.
127	   * \param engine_config The engine config.
128	   * \param trace_recorder The event trace recorder for requests.
129	   * \return The created action object.
130	   */
131	  static EngineAction BatchDraft(Array<Model> models, LogitProcessor logit_processor,
132	                                 Sampler sampler, std::vector<ModelWorkspace> model_workspaces,
133	                                 DraftTokenWorkspaceManager draft_token_workspace_manager,
134	                                 EngineConfig engine_config,
135	                                 Optional<EventTraceRecorder> trace_recorder);
136	
137	  /*!
138	   * \brief Create the action that runs one-step speculative draft proposal for
139	   * requests in the `running_queue` of engine state. Preempt low-priority requests
140	   * accordingly when it is impossible to decode all the running requests.
141	   * \param models The model to run decode in. When there are multiple
142	   * models, the `Step` function of the created action will not take effect.
143	   * \param sampler The sampler to sample new tokens.
144	   * \param model_workspaces The workspace of each model.
145	   * \param draft_token_workspace_manager The draft token workspace manager.
146	   * \param engine_config The engine config.
147	   * \param trace_recorder The event trace recorder for requests.
148	   * \return The created action object.
149	   */
150	  static EngineAction EagleBatchDraft(Array<Model> models, LogitProcessor logit_processor,
151	                                      Sampler sampler, std::vector<ModelWorkspace> model_workspaces,
152	                                      DraftTokenWorkspaceManager draft_token_workspace_manager,
153	                                      EngineConfig engine_config,
154	                                      Optional<EventTraceRecorder> trace_recorder);
155	
156	  /*!
157	   * \brief Create the action that runs one-step speculative verification for requests in the
158	   * `running_queue` of engine state. Preempt low-priority requests
159	   * accordingly when it is impossible to decode all the running requests.
160	   * \param models The model to run decode in. When there are multiple
161	   * models, the `Step` function of the created action will not take effect.
162	   * \param model_workspaces The workspace of each model.
163	   * \param draft_token_workspace_manager The draft token workspace manager.
164	   * \param sampler The sampler to sample new tokens.
165	   * \param engine_config The engine config.
166	   * \param trace_recorder The event trace recorder for requests.
167	   * \return The created action object.
168	   */
169	  static EngineAction BatchVerify(Array<Model> models, LogitProcessor logit_processor,
170	                                  Sampler sampler, std::vector<ModelWorkspace> model_workspaces,
171	                                  DraftTokenWorkspaceManager draft_token_workspace_manager,
172	                                  EngineConfig engine_config,
173	                                  Optional<EventTraceRecorder> trace_recorder);
174	
175	  /*!
176	   * \brief Create the action that runs one-step speculative verification for requests in the
177	   * `running_queue` of engine state. Preempt low-priority requests
178	   * accordingly when it is impossible to decode all the running requests.
179	   * \param models The model to run decode in. When there are multiple
180	   * models, the `Step` function of the created action will not take effect.
181	   * \param sampler The sampler to sample new tokens.
182	   * \param model_workspaces The workspace of each model.
183	   * \param draft_token_workspace_manager The draft token workspace manager.
184	   * \param engine_config The engine config.
185	   * \param trace_recorder The event trace recorder for requests.
186	   * \return The created action object.
187	   */
188	  static EngineAction EagleBatchVerify(Array<Model> models, LogitProcessor logit_processor,
189	                                       Sampler sampler,
190	                                       std::vector<ModelWorkspace> model_workspaces,
191	                                       DraftTokenWorkspaceManager draft_token_workspace_manager,
192	                                       EngineConfig engine_config,
193	                                       Optional<EventTraceRecorder> trace_recorder);
194	  /*!
195	   * \brief Create the action that executes the jump-forward decoding to predict the next tokens
196	   * according to the grammar constraint. Does nothing for the requests without grammar. The
197	   * predicted tokens will be fed to the next BatchDecode action. Retokenization may happen when
198	   * the predicted string breaks the tokenization boundary.
199	   * \param models The model to run decode in. When there are multiple
200	   * models, the `Step` function of the created action will not take effect.
201	   * \param tokenizer The tokenizer of the engine.
202	   * \param trace_recorder The event trace recorder for requests.
203	   * \return The created action object.
204	   */
205	  static EngineAction BatchJumpForward(Array<Model> models, Tokenizer tokenizer,
206	                                       Optional<EventTraceRecorder> trace_recorder);
207	
208	  /*!
209	   * \brief Create the action that first makes a decision on whether to run speculative
210	   * decoding or normal mode batch decode, and then runs the selected actions.
211	   * \param spec_decode_actions The actions for speculative decoding.
212	   * \param batch_decode_actions The actions for normal mode batch decoding.
213	   * \param engine_config The engine config.
214	   * \return The created action object
215	   */
216	  static EngineAction AutoSpecDecode(std::vector<EngineAction> spec_decode_actions,
217	                                     std::vector<EngineAction> batch_decode_actions,
218	                                     EngineConfig engine_config);
219	
220	  /*!
221	   * \brief Create the action that runs the disaggregation preparation for prefill.
222	   * \param models The underlying models whose KV cache are to be updated.
223	   * \param engine_config The engine config.
224	   * \param model_configs The config of each model.
225	   * \param trace_recorder The event trace recorder for requests.
226	   * \param request_stream_callback The stream callback function to pass the prefill
227	   * preparation result back, including the KV cache append metadata and the prefix
228	   * matched length in the prefix cache.
229	   * \return The created action object.
230	   */
231	  static EngineAction DisaggPrepareReceive(Array<Model> models, EngineConfig engine_config,
232	                                           std::vector<tvm::ffi::json::Object> model_configs,
233	                                           Optional<EventTraceRecorder> trace_recorder,
234	                                           FRequestStreamCallback request_stream_callback);
235	
236	  /*!
237	   * \brief Create the action that runs the prefill and sends KV data to remote instance.
238	   * \param models The underlying models whose KV cache are to be updated.
239	   * \param model_workspaces The workspace of each model.
240	   * \param engine_config The engine config.
241	   * \param model_configs The config of each model.
242	   * \param trace_recorder The event trace recorder for requests.
243	   * \param request_stream_callback The stream callback function to pass the prefill
244	   * preparation result back, including the KV cache append metadata and the prefix
245	   * matched length in the prefix cache.
246	   * \param device The device of the model for synchronization.
247	   * \return The created action object.
248	   */
249	  static EngineAction DisaggRemoteSend(Array<Model> models,
250	                                       std::vector<ModelWorkspace> model_workspaces,
251	                                       EngineConfig engine_config,
252	                                       std::vector<tvm::ffi::json::Object> model_configs,
253	                                       Optional<EventTraceRecorder> trace_recorder,
254	                                       FRequestStreamCallback request_stream_callback,
255	                                       Device device);
256	
257	  TVM_FFI_DEFINE_OBJECT_REF_METHODS_NULLABLE(EngineAction, ObjectRef, EngineActionObj);
258	};
259	
260	}  // namespace serve
261	}  // namespace llm
262	}  // namespace mlc

6	#ifndef MLC_LLM_SERVE_REQUEST_H_

23	using tvm::ffi::Object;
24	using tvm::ffi::ObjectRef;

26	/****************** Request ******************/
27	
28	/*!
29	 * \brief The user submitted text-generation request, which contains
30	 * a unique request id, a list of multi-modal inputs, a set of
31	 * generation configuration parameters.
32	 * \note Request is immutable and can be re-dispatched to another
33	 * node and restart the request handling on the new one.
34	 */
35	class RequestNode : public Object {
36	 public:
37	  /*!
38	   * \brief The unique identifier of the request.
39	   * Different requests should have different ids.
40	   */
41	  String id;
42	  /*!
43	   * \brief The user inputs of a request. Input may have multi-modality.
44	   * \sa data.h
45	   */
46	  Array<Data> inputs;
47	  /*!
48	   * \brief The equivalent input sequence length of the request.
49	   * "-1" means the input length is unknown due to the existence
50	   * of untokenized text data.
51	   */
52	  int prompt_tokens = -1;
53	  /*!
54	   * \brief The sampling configuration which may contain temperature,
55	   * top_p, repetition_penalty, max_gen_len, etc.
56	   */
57	  GenerationConfig generation_cfg;
58	  /*! \brief Backward reference to the request state. */
59	  Object* rstate = nullptr;
60	
61	  static void RegisterReflection() {
62	    namespace refl = tvm::ffi::reflection;
63	    refl::ObjectDef<RequestNode>();
64	  }
65	
66	  static constexpr const bool _type_has_method_sequal_reduce = false;
67	  static constexpr const bool _type_has_method_shash_reduce = false;
68	  static constexpr const bool _type_mutable = true;
69	  TVM_FFI_DECLARE_OBJECT_INFO("mlc.serve.Request", RequestNode, Object);
70	};
71	
72	class Request : public ObjectRef {
73	 public:
74	  explicit Request(String id, Array<Data> inputs, GenerationConfig generation_cfg);
75	
76	  /*!
77	   * \brief Return a request object with all text data tokenized,
78	   * and the request ID kept the same as the input one.
79	   * \param request The request to be tokenized.
80	   * \param tokenizer The tokenizer to tokenize the input data of the given request.
81	   * \return The request object whose data are tokenized.
82	   */
83	  static Request FromUntokenized(const Request& request, const Tokenizer& tokenizer);
84	
85	  TVM_FFI_DEFINE_OBJECT_REF_METHODS_NULLABLE(Request, ObjectRef, RequestNode);
86	};
87	
88	}  // namespace serve
89	}  // namespace llm
90	}  // namespace mlc
91	
92	#endif  // MLC_LLM_SERVE_REQUEST_H_

6	
7	#ifndef MLC_LLM_SERVE_MODEL_H_

9	
10	#include <tvm/ffi/extra/json.h>

14	#include "../base.h"

23	namespace mlc {

27	using tvm::Device;

31	using tvm::ffi::Shape;
32	
33	// Declare the sampler class for `Model::CreateSampler`.
34	class Sampler;
35	
36	/*!
37	 * \brief The workspace tensors that may be shared across different
38	 * calls to Model. For example, the prefill action use the `embeddings`
39	 * workspace for the concatenated embeddings of different sequences.
40	 * The workspace tensor is created by Model but owned by engine.
41	 */
42	struct ModelWorkspace {
43	  /*!
44	   * \brief The embedding tensor. It can be either an Tensor when tensor
45	   * model parallelism is not enabled, or a DRef when using tensor model parallelism.
46	   */
47	  ObjectRef embeddings{nullptr};
48	  /*!
49	   * \brief The hidden_states tensor for the current batch. It can be either an Tensor when tensor
50	   * model parallelism is not enabled, or a DRef when using tensor model parallelism.
51	   */
52	  ObjectRef hidden_states{nullptr};
53	
54	  /*!
55	   * \brief The draft token probabilities tensor for the current batch.
56	   */
57	  Tensor draft_probs{nullptr};
58	
59	  /*!
60	   * \brief The hidden_states tensor storing the hidden_states of draft tokens of all requests.
61	   */
62	  ObjectRef draft_hidden_states_storage{nullptr};
63	
64	  /*!
65	   * \brief The draft token probabilities tensor storing the probabilities of draft tokens of all
66	   * requests.
67	   */
68	  Tensor draft_probs_storage{nullptr};
69	};
70	
71	/*!
72	 * \brief The model module for LLM functions.
73	 * It runs an LLM, and has an internal KV cache that maintains
74	 * the history KV values of all processed tokens.
75	 *
76	 * It contains the following functions:
77	 *
78	 * Model related:
79	 * - "token_embed": take token ids as input and return the embeddings,
80	 * - "batch_prefill": take embedding of a single sequence
81	 * as input, forward the embedding through LLM and return the logits,
82	 * - "decode": take the embeddings of the last-committed token of an
83	 * entire batch as input, forward through LLM and return the logits
84	 * for all sequences in the batch,
85	 * - "softmax_with_temperature": take logits and temperatures, return
86	 * probabilities.
87	 *
88	 * KV cache related:
89	 * - "create_kv_cache": create the KV cache for this module,
90	 * - "add_new_sequence": add (declare) a new sequence in the KV cache,
91	 * - "remove_sequence": remove a sequence from KV cache.
92	 *
93	 * ... and some other auxiliary functions.
94	 */
95	class ModelObj : public Object {
96	 public:
97	  /*********************** Model Computation  ***********************/
98	
99	  /*!
100	   * \brief Compute embeddings for the input token ids.
101	   * When the input destination pointer is defined, it in-place writes the
102	   * embedding into the input destination array at the given offset.
103	   * Otherwise, the embeddings will be directly returned back.
104	   * \param token_ids The token ids to compute embedding for.
105	   * \param dst The destination array of the embedding lookup.
106	   * \param offset The token offset where the computed embeddings will be written
107	   * into the destination array.
108	   * \return The updated destination embedding array or the computed embeddings.
109	   * \note When `dst` is undefined, we require `offset` to be 0.
110	   */
111	  virtual ObjectRef TokenEmbed(Shape batch_token_ids, ObjectRef* dst = nullptr, int offset = 0) = 0;
112	
113	  /*!
114	   * \brief Compute embeddings for the input image.
115	   * \param image The image to compute embedding for.
116	   * \return The computed embeddings.
117	   */
118	  virtual ObjectRef ImageEmbed(const Tensor& image, ObjectRef* dst = nullptr, int offset = 0) = 0;
119	
120	  /*!
121	   * \brief Fuse the embeddings and hidden_states.
122	   * \param embeddings The embedding of the input to be prefilled.
123	   * \param previous_hidden_states The hidden_states from previous base model.
124	   * \param batch_size Batch size.
125	   * \param seq_len Sequence length.
126	   * \return The fused hidden_states.
127	   */
128	  virtual ObjectRef FuseEmbedHidden(const ObjectRef& embeddings,
129	                                    const ObjectRef& previous_hidden_states, int batch_size,
130	                                    int seq_len) = 0;
131	
132	  /*!
133	   * \brief Return if the model has lm_head so that we can get logits.
134	   */
135	  virtual bool CanGetLogits() = 0;
136	
137	  /*!
138	   * \brief Compute logits for last hidden_states.
139	   * \param last_hidden_states The last hidden_states to compute logits for.
140	   * \return The computed logits.
141	   */
142	  virtual Tensor GetLogits(const ObjectRef& last_hidden_states) = 0;
143	
144	  virtual Array<Tensor> GetMultiStepLogits(const ObjectRef& last_hidden_states) = 0;
145	
146	  /*!
147	   * \brief Batch prefill function. Embedding in, logits out.
148	   * The embedding order of sequences in `embedding_arr` follows
149	   * the order of `seq_ids`.
150	   * \param embeddings The embedding of the input to be prefilled.
151	   * \param seq_id The id of the sequence in the KV cache.
152	   * \param lengths The length of each sequence to prefill.
153	   * \return The logits for the next token.
154	   */
155	  virtual Tensor BatchPrefill(const ObjectRef& embeddings, const std::vector<int64_t>& seq_ids,
156	                              const std::vector<int>& lengths) = 0;
157	
158	  /*!
159	   * \brief Batch prefill function. Input hidden_states are computed from
160	   * input embeddings and previous hidden_states, output last hidden_states.
161	   * \param hidden_states The hidden_states of the input to be prefilled.
162	   * \param seq_id The id of the sequence in the KV cache.
163	   * \param lengths The length of each sequence to prefill.
164	   * \return The hidden_states for the next token.
165	   */
166	  virtual ObjectRef BatchPrefillToLastHidden(const ObjectRef& hidden_states,
167	                                             const std::vector<int64_t>& seq_ids,
168	                                             const std::vector<int>& lengths) = 0;
169	
170	  /*!
171	   * \brief Batch decode function. Embedding in, logits out.
172	   * The embedding order of sequences in `embeddings` follows
173	   * the order of `seq_ids`.
174	   * \param embeddings The embedding of last generated token in the entire batch.
175	   * \param seq_id The id of the sequence in the KV cache.
176	   * \return The logits for the next token for each sequence in the batch.
177	   */
178	  virtual Tensor BatchDecode(const ObjectRef& embeddings, const std::vector<int64_t>& seq_ids) = 0;
179	
180	  virtual Tensor BatchTreeDecode(const ObjectRef& embeddings, const std::vector<int64_t>& seq_ids,
181	                                 const std::vector<int>& lengths,
182	                                 const std::vector<int64_t>& token_tree_parent_ptr) = 0;
183	
184	  /*!
185	   * \brief Batch decode function. Input hidden_states are computed from
186	   * input embeddings and previous hidden_states, output last hidden_states.
187	   * \param hidden_states The hidden_states of last generated token in the entire batch.
188	   * \param seq_id The id of the sequence in the KV cache.
189	   * \return The hidden_states for the next token for each sequence in the batch.
190	   */
191	  virtual ObjectRef BatchDecodeToLastHidden(const ObjectRef& hidden_states,
192	                                            const std::vector<int64_t>& seq_ids) = 0;
193	
194	  /*!
195	   * \brief Batch verify function. Embedding in, logits out.
196	   * \param embeddings The embedding of the input to be verified.
197	   * \param seq_id The id of the sequence in the KV cache.
198	   * \param lengths The length of each sequence to verify.
199	   * \param token_tree_parent_ptr The parent pointers of the token tree.
200	   * It's size is the sum of "lengths". It contains a batch of independent trees,
201	   * one for each sequence. Parent being "-1" means the node is a root.
202	   * \return The logits for the draft token for each sequence in the batch.
203	   * \note The function runs for **every** sequence in the batch.
204	   * That is to say, it does not accept "running a verify step for a subset
205	   * of the full batch".
206	   */
207	  virtual Tensor BatchVerify(const ObjectRef& embeddings, const std::vector<int64_t>& seq_ids,
208	                             const std::vector<int>& lengths,
209	                             const std::vector<int64_t>& token_tree_parent_ptr) = 0;
210	
211	  /*!
212	   * \brief Batch verify function. Input hidden_states are computed from
213	   * input embeddings and previous hidden_states, output last hidden_states.
214	   * \param hidden_states The hidden_states of the input to be verified.
215	   * \param seq_id The id of the sequence in the KV cache.
216	   * \param lengths The length of each sequence to verify.
217	   * \param token_tree_parent_ptr The parent pointers of the token tree.
218	   * It's size is the sum of "lengths". It contains a batch of independent trees,
219	   * one for each sequence. Parent being "-1" means the node is a root.
220	   * \return The hidden_states for the draft token for each sequence in the batch.
221	   * \note The function runs for **every** sequence in the batch.
222	   * That is to say, it does not accept "running a verify step for a subset
223	   * of the full batch".
224	   */
225	  virtual ObjectRef BatchVerifyToLastHidden(const ObjectRef& hidden_states,
226	                                            const std::vector<int64_t>& seq_ids,
227	                                            const std::vector<int>& lengths,
228	                                            const std::vector<int64_t>& token_tree_parent_ptr) = 0;
229	
230	  /*********************** KV Cache Management  ***********************/
231	
232	  /*!
233	   * \brief Create the KV cache inside the model with regard to the input config.
234	   * \param page_size The number of consecutive tokens handled in each page in paged KV cache.
235	   * \param max_num_sequence The maximum number of sequences that are allowed to be
236	   * processed by the KV cache at any time.
237	   * \param max_total_sequence_length The maximum length allowed for a single sequence
238	   * in the engine.
239	   * \param prefill_chunk_size The maximum total number of tokens whose KV data
240	   * are allowed to exist in the KV cache at any time.
241	   * \param max_history_size The maximum history size for RNN state to roll back.
242	   * The KV cache does not need this.
243	   * \param prefix_cache_max_num_recycling_seqs The maximum number of recycling
244	   * sequences kept by prefix cache. For hybrid models, the RNN state needs
245	   * extra slots beyond max_num_sequence to hold these recycling sequences
246	   * simultaneously with active sequences.
247	   */
248	  virtual void CreateKVCache(int page_size, int max_num_sequence, int64_t max_total_sequence_length,
249	                             int64_t prefill_chunk_size, int max_history_size,
250	                             int prefix_cache_max_num_recycling_seqs = 0) = 0;
251	
252	  /*! \brief Add a new sequence with the given sequence id to the KV cache. */
253	  virtual void AddNewSequence(int64_t seq_id) = 0;
254	
255	  /*! \brief Fork a sequence from a given parent sequence. */
256	  virtual void ForkSequence(int64_t parent_seq_id, int64_t child_seq_id, int64_t fork_pos = -1) = 0;
257	
258	  /*! \brief Remove the given sequence from the KV cache in the model. */
259	  virtual void RemoveSequence(int64_t seq_id) = 0;
260	
261	  /*! \brief Pop out N pages from KV cache. */
262	  virtual void PopNFromKVCache(int64_t seq_id, int num_tokens) = 0;
263	
264	  /*!
265	   * \brief Commit the accepted token tree nodes to KV cache.
266	   * The unaccepted token tree node will be removed from KV cache.
267	   * This is usually used in the verification stage of speculative decoding.
268	   */
269	  virtual void CommitAcceptedTokenTreeNodesToKVCache(
270	      const std::vector<int64_t>& seq_ids, const std::vector<int64_t>& accepted_leaf_indices) = 0;
271	
272	  /*!
273	   * \brief Enabling sliding window for the given sequence.
274	   * It is a no-op if the model does not support sliding window.
275	   * \note Given this operation is tied with the underlying KV cache,
276	   * we add the function in Model interface to expose this for Engine.
277	   * This may be optimized with decoupling KV cache and Model in the future.
278	   */
279	  virtual void EnableSlidingWindowForSeq(int64_t seq_id) = 0;
280	
281	  /*! \brief Prepare for the disaggregation KV data receive for the specified sequence and length.*/
282	  virtual Shape DisaggPrepareKVRecv(int64_t seq_id, int length) = 0;
283	
284	  /*! \brief Prepare for the disaggregation KV data send for the specified sequence and length.*/
285	  virtual void DisaggMarkKVSend(int64_t seq_id, int begin_pos, Shape compressed_kv_append_metadata,
286	                                int dst_group_offset) = 0;
287	
288	  /************** Raw Info Query **************/
289	
290	  /*! \brief Return the metadata JSON object of the model. */
291	  virtual ModelMetadata GetMetadata() const = 0;
292	
293	  /*! \brief Get the number of available pages in KV cache. */
294	  virtual int GetNumAvailablePages() const = 0;
295	
296	  /*! \brief Get the current total sequence length in the KV cache. */
297	  virtual int GetCurrentTotalSequenceLength() const = 0;
298	
299	  /*********************** Utilities  ***********************/
300	
301	  /*! \brief Load the model's weight parameters, which is not loaded at construction time. */
302	  virtual void LoadParams() = 0;
303	
304	  /*!
305	   * \brief Set the maximum number of sequences to be processed for the model,
306	   * which is not initialized at construction time.
307	   */
308	  virtual void SetMaxNumSequence(int max_num_sequence) = 0;
309	
310	  /*!
311	   * \brief Set the prefill chunk size for the model,
312	   * which is not initialized at construction time.

58	class PrefixCacheObj : public Object {
59	 public:
60	  /*!
61	   * \brief Insert a new tokenized sequence into Prefix Cache.
62	   * \param seq_id The sequence ID.
63	   * \param tokens The tokens of tokenized sequence.
64	   * \param sliding_window_size The sliding window size for the sequence, -1 as sliding window
65	   * disabled.
66	   * \param attention_sink_size The attention sink size for the sequence, 0 by default.
67	   * \return The matched result.
68	   */
69	  virtual PrefixCacheMatchedResult InsertSequence(int64_t seq_id, std::vector<int32_t> tokens,
70	                                                  int sliding_window_size = -1,
71	                                                  int attention_sink_size = 0) = 0;
72	
73	  /*!
74	   * \brief Extend a sequence with new tokenized sequence suffix.
75	   * This extension might be cached and lazily committed later.
76	   * \param seq_id The sequence to be extended.
77	   * \param tokens The tokens of tokenized sequence suffix to extend.
78	   * \throw Error if the given sequence id is not valid or active.
79	   */
80	  virtual void ExtendSequence(int64_t seq_id, const std::vector<int32_t>& tokens) = 0;
81	
82	  /*! \brief Commit the cached sequence extension from "ExtendSequence". */
83	  virtual void CommitSequenceExtention() = 0;
84	
85	  /*!
86	   * \brief Roll back a sequence by number of tokens.
87	   * \param seq_id The sequence ID for index.
88	   * \param num_tokens The number of tokens to be rolled back.
89	   * \throw Error if the given sequence id is not valid or active.
90	   */
91	  virtual void RollBackSequence(int64_t seq_id, size_t num_tokens) = 0;
92

97	   * \param seq_id The sequence to be recycled.
98	   * \param lazy The flag if the sequence should be removed lazily or intermediary.
99	   * \throw Error if the given sequence id is not valid.
100	   */

102	
103	  /*!

107	   freed successfully.

29	/*!
30	 * \brief The base class of runtime sampler.
31	 * Its main function is `BatchSampleTokensWithProbBeforeTopP`, which takes a batch of
32	 * logits and corresponding configuration, and sample one token
33	 * for each instance of the batch.
34	 */
35	class SamplerObj : public Object {
36	 public:
37	  /*!
38	   * \brief Renormalize the input batch of probability distributions with top p values.
39	   * \param probs_on_device The batch of prob distributions before normalization.
40	   * \param sample_indices Specifying which request we will sample for
41	   * in i-th output for the sampling later on.
42	   * The output result of the sampling will be as follow:
43	   *   result[i] = sample_from(prob_on_device[sample_indices[i],:], generation_config[i]));
44	   * For renormalization, the sample indices are used for determine the top-p grouping.
45	   * \param request_ids The id of each request.
46	   * \param generation_cfg The generation config of each request in the input batch.
47	   * \return The renormalized probability distributions, residing on device
48	   * if the sampler is GPU sampler, or on host if the sampler is CPU sampler.
49	   */
50	  virtual Tensor BatchRenormalizeProbsByTopP(Tensor probs_on_device,                  //
51	                                             const std::vector<int>& sample_indices,  //
52	                                             const Array<String>& request_ids,        //
53	                                             const Array<GenerationConfig>& generation_cfg) = 0;
54	
55	  /*!
56	   * \brief Sample tokens from the input batch of prob distribution on device.
57	   * The input prob distributions are not yet applied with top-p.
58	   * \param probs_on_device The prob distributions on GPU to sample tokens from.
59	   * \param sample_indices Specifying which request we should sample for
60	   * in i-th output. The output result is sample as follow:
61	   *   result[i] = sample_from(prob_on_device[sample_indices[i],:], generation_config[i]));
62	   * \param request_ids The id of each request.
63	   * \param generation_cfg The generation config of each request
64	   * in the input batch.
65	   * \param rngs The random number generator of each sequence.
66	   * \return The batch of sampling results, which contain the sampled token id
67	   * and other probability info.
68	   */
69	  virtual std::vector<SampleResult> BatchSampleTokensWithProbBeforeTopP(
70	      Tensor probs_on_device,                         //
71	      const std::vector<int>& sample_indices,         //
72	      const Array<String>& request_ids,               //
73	      const Array<GenerationConfig>& generation_cfg,  //
74	      const std::vector<RandomGenerator*>& rngs) = 0;
75	
76	  /*!
77	   * \brief Sample tokens from the input batch of prob distribution on device.
78	   * The input prob distributions are already applied with top-p.
79	   * \param probs The prob distributions.
80	   * It resides on GPU if the sampler is GPU sampler, or on host if hte sampler is CPU sampler.
81	   * \param sample_indices Specifying which request we should sample for
82	   * in i-th output. The output result is sample as follow:
83	   *   result[i] = sample_from(prob_on_device[sample_indices[i],:], generation_config[i]));
84	   * \param request_ids The id of each request.
85	   * \param generation_cfg The generation config of each request
86	   * in the input batch.
87	   * \param rngs The random number generator of each sequence.
88	   * \return The batch of sampling results, which contain the sampled token id
89	   * and other probability info.
90	   */
91	  virtual std::vector<SampleResult> BatchSampleTokensWithProbAfterTopP(
92	      Tensor probs,                                   //
93	      const std::vector<int>& sample_indices,         //
94	      const Array<String>& request_ids,               //
95	      const Array<GenerationConfig>& generation_cfg,  //
96	      const std::vector<RandomGenerator*>& rngs) = 0;
97	
98	  /*!
99	   * \brief Verify draft tokens generated by small models in the large model
100	   * in speculative decoding. The input corresponds to a batch of sequences.
101	   * The input prob distributions are already applied with top-p.
102	   * \param probs The prob distributions on GPU to sample tokens from.
103	   * It resides on GPU if the sampler is GPU sampler, or on host if hte sampler is CPU sampler.
104	   * \param request_ids The id of each request.
105	   * \param cum_verify_lengths The cumulative draft lengths to verify of all sequences.
106	   * \param generation_cfg The generation config of each request
107	   * in the input batch.
108	   * \param rngs The random number generator of each sequence.
109	   * \param draft_output_tokens The draft tokens generated by the small model for
110	   * each sequence.
111	   * \param token_tree_parent_ptr The parent pointer of the token tree.
112	   * \param draft_probs_on_device The probability distribution computed from the
113	   * small model for each sequence. Concatenated tensor of shape (total_verify_length, vocab_size).
114	   * It includes the slot for the last committed token that has undefined probablity value.
115	   * \return The list of accepted tokens for each request and the index of the last accepted tree
116	   * node for each request.
117	   */
118	  virtual std::pair<std::vector<std::vector<SampleResult>>, std::vector<int>>
119	  BatchVerifyDraftTokensWithProbAfterTopP(
120	      Tensor probs, const Array<String>& request_ids, const std::vector<int>& cum_verify_lengths,
121	      const Array<GenerationConfig>& generation_cfg, const std::vector<RandomGenerator*>& rngs,
122	      const std::vector<std::vector<SampleResult>>& draft_output_tokens,
123	      const std::vector<int64_t>& token_tree_parent_ptr, Tensor draft_probs_on_device) = 0;
124	
125	  static void RegisterReflection() {
126	    namespace refl = tvm::ffi::reflection;
127	    refl::ObjectDef<SamplerObj>();
128	  }
129	
130	  static constexpr const bool _type_has_method_sequal_reduce = false;
131	  static constexpr const bool _type_has_method_shash_reduce = false;
132	  static constexpr const bool _type_mutable = true;
133	  TVM_FFI_DECLARE_OBJECT_INFO("mlc.serve.Sampler", SamplerObj, Object);
134	};

137	 public:
138	  /*! * \brief Create a CPU sampler. */
139	  static Sampler CreateCPUSampler(Optional<EventTraceRecorder> trace_recorder);
140	  /*!
141	   * \brief Create a GPU sampler.
142	   * \param max_num_sample The max number of samples to sample at a time.
143	   * \param vocab_size The model's vocabulary size.
144	   * \param ft The packed function table.
145	   * \param device The device that the model runs on.
146	   * \param trace_recorder The event trace recorder.
147	   */
148	  static Sampler CreateGPUSampler(int max_num_sample, int vocab_size, FunctionTable* ft,
149	                                  DLDevice device, Optional<EventTraceRecorder> trace_recorder);
150	
151	  /*! \brief Check if the given device supports GPU sampling. */
152	  static bool SupportGPUSampler(Device device) {
153	    return device.device_type == DLDeviceType::kDLCUDA ||
154	           device.device_type == DLDeviceType::kDLVulkan ||
155	           device.device_type == DLDeviceType::kDLMetal;
156	  }
157	
158	  TVM_FFI_DEFINE_OBJECT_REF_METHODS_NULLABLE(Sampler, ObjectRef, SamplerObj);
159	};

## C++ Serve Engine Structural Map for Learning Handbook

### 1. Main Engine Entry Points

**Key files:**
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/cpp/serve/engine.h` - Core Engine interface
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/cpp/serve/threaded_engine.h` - Threaded wrapper
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/cpp/serve/engine_state.h` - State container

**Classes and relationships:**

- **Engine** (abstract base class): Main interface with 3 categories:
  - Engine management: `Create()`, `Reset()`, `Empty()`
  - Request management: `AddRequest()`, `AbortRequest()`, `AbortAllRequests()`
  - Action loop: `Step()` - the core action-driven interface

- **ThreadedEngine**: Wrapper around Engine that runs a background request processing loop on a standalone thread. Provides thread-safe `AddRequest`/`AbortRequest` from other threads. Uses instruction queue with mutex/cv for synchronization.

- **EngineState** (ObjectRef): Holds all engine state:
  - `running_queue` - requests currently being processed
  - `waiting_queue` - requests not yet started
  - `request_states` - map of RequestState for each request
  - `prefix_cache` - KV cache prefix sharing
  - `metrics` - runtime metrics
  - `id_manager` - internal ID allocation
  - `postproc_workspace` - reusable post-process structures

**Architecture model: Event-Action / Request-driven loop**
1. External requests arrive via `AddRequest()` → go to `waiting_queue`
2. Engine `Step()` invokes Actions (prefill/decode/verify)
3. Each Action's `Step(estate)` method:
   - Analyzes current engine state
   - Invokes model functions (batched prefill/decode)
   - Runs sampler to sample tokens
   - Updates engine state
4. Post-processing returns finished requests via callback

---

### 2. engine_actions/ Directory

**Base interface:** `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/cpp/serve/engine_actions/action.h`

**Action interface** (`EngineActionObj` abstract base):
- Single core method: `virtual Array<Request> Step(EngineState estate) = 0`
- Takes engine state as input, returns processed requests
- Actions invoke model functions, run sampler, update state

**All Actions (factory methods in EngineAction class):**

| Action | One-line purpose |
|--------|------------------|
| **NewRequestPrefill** | Prefills requests from `waiting_queue`, moves them to `running_queue` |
| **EagleNewRequestPrefill** | EAGLE speculative decoding variant of new request prefill |
| **BatchDecode** | One-step decode for all running requests, handles preemption if needed |
| **BatchDraft** | Speculative draft proposal using small model for running requests |
| **EagleBatchDraft** | EAGLE variant of speculative draft proposal |
| **BatchVerify** | Verifies speculative draft tokens against large model |
| **EagleBatchVerify** | EAGLE variant of draft verification |
| **BatchJumpForward** | Grammar-constrained jump-forward decoding (predicts next tokens per grammar) |
| **AutoSpecDecode** | Meta-action that decides between speculative vs normal decode |
| **DisaggPrepareReceive** | Disaggregation: prepares KV cache for receiving remote prefill data |
| **DisaggRemoteSend** | Disaggregation: runs prefill locally and sends KV data to remote instance |

**Base class:** `BatchPrefillBaseActionObj` - shared base for prefill-style actions

---

### 3. Request Lifecycle

**Key files:**
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/cpp/serve/request.h` - User request definition
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/cpp/serve/request_state.h` - Request state tracking

**Request** (immutable, user-submitted):
- `id` - unique request identifier
- `inputs` - multi-modal input data (text, tokens, images)
- `prompt_tokens` - input length (-1 if unknown/untokenized)
- `generation_cfg` - sampling params (temperature, top_p, max_tokens, etc.)

**RequestState** (engine's view of request progress):
- Contains multiple `RequestStateEntry` instances (tree structure for parallel generations via `n > 1`)
- Root entry (index 0) = prompt prefix
- Child entries = each parallel generation branch

**RequestStateEntry** (single generation's state):
- `status` - kPending/kAlive/kFinished
- `mstates` - Array<RequestModelState> (one per model)
- `rng` - random number generator
- `stop_str_handler` - stop string detection
- `next_callback_token_pos` - stream callback position

**RequestModelState** (per-model state):
- `request` - back-reference to Request
- `internal_id` - physical index in running queue (-1 if not running)
- `model_id` - which model this state is for
- `committed_tokens` - final tokens that won't change
- `draft_output_tokens` - speculative tokens (for verification)
- `inputs` - remaining input to prefill
- `prefilled_inputs` - already prefilled (for prefix cache)
- `grammar_matcher` - optional grammar constraint state

**Flow:** `waiting_queue` → `running_queue` → finished/removed
- Preemption moves requests from running back to waiting

---

### 4. KV Cache & Prefix Cache

**Key files:**
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/cpp/serve/prefix_cache.h` - Prefix cache interface
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/cpp/serve/radix_tree.h` - Radix tree data structure

**PrefixCache** (abstract base `PrefixCacheObj`):
- Stores token sequences for reuse across requests
- Modes: Radix tree (enabled) or no-op (disabled)
- Key operations:
  - `InsertSequence()` - insert new sequence, returns match result
  - `ExtendSequence()` - add suffix, lazy commit
  - `RecycleSequence()` - mark for reuse (not immediate removal)
  - `TryFreeMemory()` - evict oldest recycling sequence if needed

**PrefixCacheMatchedResult** - describes how to reuse KV cache:
- `prefilled_offset` - matched prefix length
- `forked_seq_id` - sequence to fork from
- `reused_seq_id` - finished sequence to reuse
- `reused_seq_pop_last_tokens` - tokens to pop from reused sequence

**PagedRadixTree** - paged radix tree implementation:
- Stores sequences as token paths in tree
- Operations: `MatchPrefix()`, `ForkSequence()`, `ExtendSequence()`, `RollBackSequence()`
- `FreeCapacity()` - remaining token capacity

**How prefix caching works:**
1. New request arrives → check radix tree for longest common prefix
2. If match found: fork from existing sequence or reuse finished sequence
3. Extend sequence as tokens generated
4. Recycle finished sequences for future reuse

---

### 5. Model Loading & Configuration

**Key files:**
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/cpp/serve/model.h` - Model interface
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/cpp/serve/function_table.h` - Compiled function table
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/cpp/serve/config.h` - Engine/model config

**Model** (`ModelObj` abstract base): Runtime LLM module with KV cache

**Model computation functions:**
- `TokenEmbed()` - compute embeddings for token IDs
- `ImageEmbed()` - compute embeddings for images
- `BatchPrefill()` - embedding → logits for new sequences
- `BatchDecode()` - embedding → logits for running batch
- `BatchVerify()` - verify draft tokens (speculative decoding)
- `GetLogits()` - get logits from hidden states

**KV cache management:**
- `CreateKVCache()` - initialize with page_size, max_num_sequence, etc.
- `AddNewSequence()` - declare new sequence
- `ForkSequence()` - fork from parent at position
- `RemoveSequence()` - delete sequence
- `CommitAcceptedTokenTreeNodesToKVCache()` - for speculative verification

**FunctionTable:** Holds compiled TVM functions
- `Init()` - loads model library, initializes functions
- Functions: embed, prefill, decode, verify, softmax, KV cache ops, sampling ops
- Supports distributed execution via disco (Session)

**EngineConfig** - from config.h:
- Model paths, device settings
- KV cache config (page_size, max_num_sequence, prefill_chunk_size)
- Speculative decoding settings
- Grammar execution mode
- Disaggregation config

**Loading flow:**
1. `Model::Create(reload_lib_path, model_path, model_config, device, ...)`
2. FunctionTable loads library and initializes functions
3. Model creates KV cache via `CreateKVCache()`
4. Engine creates actions using Model + FunctionTable

---

### 6. Sampler (CPU vs GPU)

**Key file:** `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/cpp/serve/sampler/sampler.h`

**Sampler** (`SamplerObj` abstract base): Samples tokens from probability distributions

**Key methods:**
- `BatchRenormalizeProbsByTopP()` - apply top-p filtering
- `BatchSampleTokensWithProbBeforeTopP()` - sample from raw probs
- `BatchSampleTokensWithProbAfterTopP()` - sample from filtered probs
- `BatchVerifyDraftTokensWithProbAfterTopP()` - verify draft tokens in speculative decoding

**Two implementations:**
- **CPUSampler** (`cpu_sampler.cc`): Sampling on CPU (fallback)
- **GPUSampler** (`gpu_sampler.cc`): GPU sampling via CUDA/Vulkan/Metal (uses function_table)

**Selection:** `Sampler::SupportGPUSampler(device)` checks device type

---

### 7. Metrics & Event Trace Recording

**Key files:**
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/cpp/serve/metrics.h` - Runtime metrics
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/mlc-ai-mlc-llm/cpp/serve/event_trace_recorder.h` - Event tracing

**Metrics structures:**
- `TimeCost` - tracks mean time (sum/count, with warmup)
- `SpecDecodeMetrics` - draft/accept counts for speculative decoding
- `RequestMetrics` - per-request metrics (prefill_time, decode_time, token throughput)
- `EngineMetrics` - aggregated engine stats

**EventTraceRecorder:** Records events for Chrome Trace Format
- `AddEvent(request_id, "start xxx")` / `AddEvent(request_id, "finish xxx")` / `AddEvent(request_id, "yyy")`
- `DumpJSON()` - export Chrome trace format
- Macro: `RECORD_EVENT(trace_recorder, request_ids, event)`

---

### Most Important 12 Files (for lectures):

| Priority | File | Purpose |
|----------|------|---------|
| 1 | `engine.h` | Core Engine interface - entry point |
| 2 | `engine_state.h` | State container - understand what engine tracks |
| 3 | `engine_actions/action.h` | Action abstraction - event-action model |
| 4 | `threaded_engine.h` | Threading wrapper - production interface |
| 5 | `request.h` | User request structure |
| 6 | `request_state.h` | Request lifecycle & state tracking |
| 7 | `model.h` | Model computation & KV cache interface |
| 8 | `prefix_cache.h` | Prefix caching for KV reuse |
| 9 | `engine.cc` | Engine::Create implementation - initialization flow |
| 10 | `engine_actions/action_commons.h` | Action utilities - preemption, post-processing |
| 11 | `sampler/sampler.h` | Token sampling interface |
| 12 | `config.h` | Configuration structures |
